content: Treatment approaches vary by substance. Opioid Use Disorder: Medication for OUD (MOUD) first-line—methadone, buprenorphine, or extended-release naltrexone reduce mortality and improve outcomes; combine with counseling/behavioral therapies. Alcohol Use Disorder: naltrexone, acamprosate, disulfiram; motivational interviewing, CBT, contingency management. Tobacco: NRT, varenicline, bupropion plus behavioral support. Stimulant use: contingency management has strongest evidence; no FDA-approved medications yet. Benzodiazepines: gradual tapering. Treatment settings range from outpatient to residential based on severity. Recovery support services, peer support, and addressing co-occurring mental health conditions essential for long-term success.
